reviews.review_msg: Donot take this class
Really boring  seminars.

Now that I'm taking my last semester at SFU, I've come to realize that the material I was assigned in Bob's class was EXTREMELY useful. His syllabus follows a very organized outline that will give you an in depth understanding of whatever topic is being talked about. Don't be late for class or on papers-- has a strict no excuse policy
The lecture's boring and such a hard marker. I will never take his class again.
I'd retake this course in a minute.  So interesting!  Expectations were extremely clear.  Everything was set out on the first day of classes.  Lectures were recorded.  21st century learning!
Excellent professor.  Very clearly set out expectations.  One of the best professors and one of the best courses I took in my undergraduate degree.  Very fair marker.

Professor Hackett is so knowledgeable on this subject, and his passion to always discover more about the changing media field totally comes through his lectures. He's not the easiest marker, but if you attend class and work hard, you can be successful. Also, he's completely open to feedback, which is great!

This class was fascinating. I learned so much, and yes, bob's lectures can be dry at times, but if you engage and concentrate in his class you will learn so much from this brilliant man! Don't worry about doing the readings as much as attending the lectures and taking detailed notes.
UGH! Such a hard marker.. out of our class of over 20 people, only ONE got an A+ and one got an A-. If you value high marks avoid this class at all costs..
Even gave us a freakin checklist for the final take home exam, very itty gritty and overly stubborn about the most minor things. Does not trust his students either, please please get out of SFU.
DRY lectures. He makes sure you go to lectures with his "quick writes". Hard marker ugh! NEVER will I ever take a class with him again. GPA dropper for sure.
He is an extremely hard marker and boring. Unless you like to talk in class, don't take this course at all. Gpa dropper for sure.
DO NOT ever take CMNS 437 with him not only will it bring your GPA down but a lot of work is required weekly with the reading journals.
He's professional and expected a lot for students. Interesting topic but hard to learn so. HARD MARKER
I don't think he is as boring as everyone says, but he is very serious and comes across as slightly arrogant. Don't volunteer your opinion if you fear him rejecting it.
A very serious prof indeed. Very VERY boring and definitely a hard marker.
Literally the most boring professor I have ever had, and a bit smug at times.  I don't comprehend how we could make such an interesting topic so unbearable.  Please retire.

A really nice guy.  Takes your complaints really seriously (aka he actually does something about it).  He's a little too left for my tastes.  Really smart and knows everything there is to know about cmns.  Hard marker at times.

I found him to be friendly, helpful, somewhat dry (he does not use powerpoint in his lectures, you will only get the odd overhead now and then) but very thorough.  Interesting course content, definitely an advanced kind of lecture. Overall not bad!

Yes, Bob Hackett can be dry and pofessorial, but after all, he is a professor. I found his lectures very intelligent and cohesive, and his knowledge level extremely high. He's sort of an advanced professor, requiring advanced students.
Looking back on it? I should have taken speed before his lectures.  Decent content, Boring lectures, but the guy is really dry.
Took this course, and was the more boring thing I've ever taken. Drying paint is much more interesting and informative.  I'm in media relations now, and have not found his stuff useful one bit.

At the final exam he informed us that a few students had complained that there was too much to study so he decided to take out the MAP out of the final exam. This was unfair because only a few students were aware of this and the rest of us studied it.
attendance in lecture? what the hell? truly a boring lecturer.. not a good professor to talk to in person. VERY stubborn
Runs an interesting participatory lecture
No Comments
